author: claude
content:
Landed as DRAFT PR #1209 (https://github.com/openwpm/OpenWPM/pull/1209), branch fix/geckodriver-fifo-sigabrt, base master.

Fix: backed FirefoxLogInterceptor with a REGULAR FILE instead of a FIFO. Regular-file writes never raise EPIPE, so geckodriver can no longer be aborted by reader/teardown ordering — removes the SIGABRT-on-teardown failure mode entirely (not just narrowing the race).

Changes (3 files):
- selenium_firefox.py: tempfile.mkstemp instead of mktempfifo; interceptor tails the file and stops on an explicit threading.Event (stop()), draining remaining lines then unlinking; removed unused mktempfifo helper. daemon flag retained.
- deploy_firefox.py: log_output -> regular file; return interceptor to caller.
- browser_manager.py: run_impl finally-block calls webdriver_interceptor.stop() (after driver already quit/killed, so no write can race).

Verification:
- Deterministic OS-level proof: FIFO write after reader-close -> EPIPE; regular-file write after reader-close -> succeeds (root cause confirmed + fix removes it).
- Session-driven direct-Selenium repro (geckodriver 0.37.0 / FF 152): regular-file interceptor -> geckodriver survives reader teardown mid-session, log lines captured, clean exit. The exact FIFO abort window is timing-finicky to trigger on demand; OS-level proof + structural argument cover correctness, full validation on CI.
- pytest -m pyonly green; mypy clean on both deploy files; black/isort clean; pre-commit green.

Note: deeper cause is upstream (geckodriver/Rust should treat broken-pipe-on-stdout as clean exit, not panic-to-abort) — flagged in PR for a separate Mozilla report. Closes #1208. Not merged; draft for human review.
